items.q: What is produced in anaerobic respiration in human muscle cells?
items.correct: Lactic acid (lactate) and a small amount of ATP — no CO2 or water in this pathway.
items.misconception: Anaerobic respiration produces ethanol and CO2 in humans.
items.random_wrong: Anaerobic respiration produces oxygen as a waste product.
items.why: Students confuse anaerobic respiration in yeast (alcohol + CO2) with the human form (lactic acid).

items.correct: An undifferentiated cell capable of dividing to produce more stem cells or differentiating into specialised cell types.
items.misconception: Stem cells are taken only from embryos and cannot be found in adults.
items.random_wrong: Stem cells are simply cells that haven't grown big enough yet.
items.why: Media coverage of embryonic stem cells overshadows adult stem cells, so students think 'stem cell' = 'embryo cell'.

items.correct: No — humans and chimpanzees share a common ancestor; both have evolved from it.
items.misconception: Humans evolved directly from chimpanzees, which is why chimps still exist as a less-evolved version.
items.random_wrong: Humans evolved from monkeys but chimps evolved from gorillas.
items.why: The phrase 'descended from apes' and linear evolution diagrams encourage 'A turned into B' thinking, missing the branching tree model.

items.q: Which process converts N₂ to ammonia in legume root nodules?
items.why: It's Nitrogen fixation. “Nitrification” is the classic near-miss here; knowing why it's wrong is worth as much as knowing the right answer.

items.q: Eutrophication is caused by excess of which nutrient?
items.why: The answer examiners want is: Nitrate. “Carbon” is the distractor this question was built around — if it tempted you, that's the exact gap to close.

items.q: If pre-industrial atmospheric CO₂ was 280 ppm and now 420 ppm, what is the percentage increase to the nearest %?
items.why: Exact answer: 50. Estimation gets you near it; only the anchored number survives the exam hall.
